response: The life of this influential art historian was deeply rooted in the study of the Renaissance, a period that defined her scholarly career. She dedicated years to compiling a comprehensive catalogue of paintings from the Italian Renaissance, ensuring that every work was documented with precision. Her groundbreaking research into the Renaissance revealed new insights about the artists who shaped the era, and she published a second catalogue to track the provenance of these masterpieces. As her reputation grew, she continued to explore the complexities of the Renaissance, producing a third catalogue that became a standard reference for scholars worldwide. Her legacy in art history is unmatched.
